scopes.rationale: User profile claims (id, name, email, group membership) are embedded in the access-token JWT. Decode the token instead of asking for extra scopes or calling a userinfo endpoint.

typescript.rule: client_id is the app name itself — the same snake_case identifier that anchors app.<app_name>.

tokenStorage: platform-appropriate secure storage (browser: in-memory or secure cookie; mobile: Keychain/Keystore; desktop: OS credential store; CLI: file with restrictive perms).

User.note: id is a UUID. Belongs to exactly one Group.

platformClasses.rule: Never redefine User or Group in any app.<app_name> namespace. Reference users by storing their UUID on a *Id field; record the link in the References registry as pointing to User._id.

authorization.enforcement: backend, on every object, action, and view
authorization.rule: Do not reimplement policy on the client. Surface backend authorization rejections in the UI rather than gating actions client-side.

ux.rule: Always include a user-initiated entry point to the login flow (Login button, sign-in screen, login subcommand). An unauthenticated user must never be stuck without an obvious way to start authenticating.
